economic_notes: Paul First Nation operates numerous business enterprises including Alberta Groups LP, Paul Band Business Trust, Paul First Nation Cascade LP, Paul First Nation Renewable Energy LP, Weh-Hnim-Nay Energy LP, Wihneme Industrial Closure Services LP, PFN Development Corporation, PFN Land Holdings, PFN Business Centre, PFN Construction, PFN Fleet, Paul First Nation Industry Relations Corporation, and Wapta Mnodai Developments Ltd. (Ironhead Golf Course). Combined enterprise revenue was $11,162,262 for 2023.
notes: Audit is qualified on two grounds: (1) asset retirement obligations have not been estimated or recorded per PS 3280 adopted April 1, 2022; (2) insufficient evidence obtained regarding carrying amount of investments in related First Nation entities including Paul Band Property Management Corp which had no audited financials. 14 enterprises accounted for by modified equity method. 1 subsidiary (Wapta Mnodai Developments Ltd./Ironhead Golf Course) fully consolidated. Restricted cash of $19,598,864 includes externally restricted Provincial funds of $955,679 and internally restricted funds across multiple programs. CMHC Replacement Reserve Fund underfunded by $670,891. Trust funds total $7,213,334 held by Government of Canada and in settlement/community trusts. Net financial assets turned negative from $3,939,451 to ($2,991,368) during the year. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $27,394,157 + CMHC $220,274 + FNDF $790,214 + ISETS $764,128 = $29,168,773. OSR calculated as total revenue $33,100,581 minus government transfers $29,168,773 = $3,931,808.
